title: Forward-Only Latent Tyre-State Filter
summary: Recent F1 work models tyre degradation as a latent state with pit stops as state resets, which is closer to the real decision variable than static TyreLife ratios.
rationale: Recent F1 work models tyre degradation as a latent state with pit stops as state resets, which is closer to the real decision variable than static TyreLife ratios. Your best pipeline already uses many hand-built wear proxies, so the next gain may come from replacing some of them with a filtered hidden wear/pace estimate updated only from past laps.
implementation_hint: For each Year-Race-Driver stint, fit a simple forward filter on lap-time residuals and degradation (EWMA/Kalman-style), reset on PitStop=1, and expose features like latent_wear, latent_wear_slope, residual_vs_expected_wear, and uncertainty. Compute all parameters on train folds only.
expected_effect: Better separation of true pit-window pressure from noisy lap-level swings, especially when raw TyreLife and Cumulative_Degradation disagree.
risk: Easy to leak if the filter is smoothed bidirectionally or normalized with full-race statistics; keep it strictly causal.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
